title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | June 30, 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Good long-term bet but won't outperform hugely; buy on correction.
recommendations.detail: Already trading above 50 PE and near 52-week highs, now modestly corrected to ~1,400+. Hospital sector has good FII interest pushing PEs higher. He'd pick it up on a dip to 1,275-1,300.
Fell along with other tech majors on the AI overhang, but some stability came after Nilekani's AGM speech. A good long-term bet, though not expected to give greatly outperforming returns.

recommendations.note: Long-term good returns as loan book builds.
recommendations.detail: Corrected after listing and has now stabilised; its loan portfolio is building up. For the long term it might give good returns.

recommendations.note: High-yield InvIT income play; review every 6 months.
recommendations.detail: A good highest-dividend InvIT with ~15.7% yield (anywhere above 12% likely), but not a 15-year instrument - typical InvIT life is 5-10 years and it may not exist after 10 years. Do it as income, review at least every 6 months, and diversify across 2-3 InvITs/REITs.

recommendations.note: Second income InvIT to diversify into (~12% yield, quarterly).
recommendations.detail: Suggested alongside Capital Infra Trust for diversification. Around 12% dividend yield works out and they pay quarterly dividends.
Holder is up ~6x from 292. 'The word sell will never come from my mouth for Laurus.' Pharma is strong on a firm dollar; Laurus is up ~10% over the last month and crossed 1,500.

recommendations.note: Valuations stretched but strong momentum; add on dips.
recommendations.detail: Valuations look a bit stretched, but there is strong momentum and emerging buying interest. Management gave huge guidance last quarter which drove the rally; watch this quarter's execution. Add more on dips after the Q1 results.

recommendations.note: Extremely strong chart but too expensive to chase now.
recommendations.detail: 52-week chart is extremely strong (higher-top/higher-bottom, jumped ~to 3,500 from May-last-week to June-first-week), but it is a not-cheap ~120-PE stock and any correction will be severe. Given at 2,000 at the Hyderabad workshop (now ~doubled). Don't chase here - be afraid to buy after such a jump; wait for dips.

recommendations.note: Ambani-owned, weak counter with AI/quick-commerce headwinds.
recommendations.detail: Ambani-acquired stocks trade weak (cf. Sterling Wilson); previous high (bought at 750) is doubtful. Search-dependent models face pressure from AI tools and quick commerce. A short-term weak counter - exit at 580-590 in rallies unless it aligns with Reliance Retail. Rule of thumb: sell on Ambani takeovers.

recommendations.note: Micro SME stock near 52-week low; not for small investors.
recommendations.detail: An SME stock with an extremely small market cap (under 70 crore), already near its 52-week low. Not a good choice and not advisable for a small investor. Better stocks exist; buy such names only after a proven track record - otherwise avoid.

recommendations.note: Weak since listing; switch to parent SBI.
recommendations.detail: Holder at 860, now 550 (-30%). Card companies lack valuations in India versus US peers (Visa); the stock has traded continuously weak right from listing. Better to shift to parent company SBI. Expect a relief rally at results - exit around 630-640.
Sugar companies are not performing and results aren't coming as expected. With a weak monsoon the crushing season is likely to be poor too. As of now it is avoidable - exit in rallies.
